markdownRemark.html:
If GitHub repo already exists

Clone repo to local

How to add an existing project to GitHub repo using command line

Create repository on GitHub

  • Copy the SSH link (remote URL)

Open terminal (bash)

Initialize the local directory as a Git repository

$ git init

Add the files in your new local repository

$ git add .

Commit the files you staged

$ git commit -m "[whatever you want]"

Add the URL for the remote repo

$ git remote add origin [remote repo url]

Push the changes

$ git push origin master

How to create a new branch and push to it

Create new branch

git checkout -b feature_branch_name

Edit, add, and commit files

Push to remote repository

git push -u origin feature_branch_name
